title: Repository snapshots for LASSO-CACTI overview paper
author: Gustafson, William
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The following code repositories were used to generate the simulations, analyses, and plots presented in the LASSO-CACTI overview paper. This folder contains snapshots of the codes that reproduce the results, to the best of our knowledge. Some figures were developed further in software such as Adobe Illustrator to adjust line thicknesses, panel layout, and labeling; these edits maintained the scientific integrity of the plots. Questions can be addressed to lasso@arm.gov. LASSO-CACTI stands for the Large-Eddy Simulation (LES) Atmospheric Radiation Measurement (ARM) Symbiotic Simulation and Observation scenario for the US Department of Energy ARM Cloud, Aerosol, and Complex Terrain Interactions (CACTI) field campaign. CACTI occurred in the region of Córdoba, Argentina from late 2018 through early 2019. 
 The paper associated with this data is in review as of November 2025: 
 Gustafson, W. I., A. M. Vogelmann, S. E. Giangrande, M. M. Delgado, S. Endo, J. D. Rausch, E. K. Schuman, A. C. Varble, and H. Xiao, 2025: The LASSO-CACTI library of deep convection simulations for Córdoba, Argentina: Overview of mesoscale and large-eddy simulations.  Earth Space Sci. , in review. 
 More information about LASSO-CACTI can be obtained from its online technical documentation at  https://lasso-cacti-doc.arm.gov/latest/index.html . 
 Contact: William I. Gustafson Jr.          Principal Investigator for LASSO          Pacific Northwest National Laboratory,          lasso@arm.gov or william.gustafson@pnnl.gov 
   
 Software repositories used for the paper 
 Each repository is stored in a separate folder within the zip file for this DOI. The first line of each "repository block", below, provides the repository name and the folder name in which to find that repository. 
  WRF-Hydro: wrf_hydro_nwm_public Repository cloning location:  https://github.com/NCAR/wrf_hydro_nwm_public.git  Code version: 5.1.2 Repository tag: 5.1.2 License:  https://github.com/NCAR/wrf_hydro_nwm_public/blob/v5.1.2/LICENSE.txt  
 Code to ingest ERA5-Land data into WRF-Hydro: wrfhydro_forcing_preparation Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/wrfhydro_forcing_preparation.git  Repository commit hash: d11c7ea90713499e91a902e07c82dd3795bbb55b License: BSD 3-Clause License 
 LASSO-CACTI Modified Version of WRF Preprocessing System: lasso-wps-cacti Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/lasso-wps-cacti.git  Repository commit hash: a5ef10601dae7ec099009fa0af901d78ab53f655 License:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/lasso-wrf-cacti/-/blob/main/LICENSE.txt  
 LASSO-CACTI Modified Version of WRF model: lasso-wrf-cacti Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/lasso-wrf-cacti.git  Repository co https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/lasso-wrf-cacti/-/blob/main/LICENSE.txt mmit hash: 3e761eacb32850fb656c6ee70dd317688c0bddc0 License: https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/lasso-wrf-cacti/-/blob/main/LICENSE.txt 
 Code to subset WRF output files: subsetwrf Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/subsetwrf.git  Repository commit hash: 5221e0747f77dfd4c24bf592b3e42618214a73bc License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code for Tb skill scoring for Meso simulations: cacti_post_proc_Tb_meso Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/cacti_post_proc.git  Repository commit hash: acac2079e323a3e49745e31c381d839078580dbc License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code for Tb skill scoring for LES simulations: cacti_post_proc_Tb_les Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/cacti_post_proc.git  Repository commit hash: 4bd39632b71674276c74a9032300c6814526a68d License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code for ETH skill scoring for Meso simulations: cacti_post_proc_eth_meso Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/cacti_post_proc_eth.git  Repository commit hash: 2ff6d4d084c5e071029cc829cb1f51a78697b6d6 License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code for ETH skill scoring for LES simulations: cacti_post_proc_eth_les Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/cacti_post_proc_eth.git  Repository commit hash: 98e32f544048f9110a75fe4deff4f97beba3a968 License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code for precipitation skill scoring: rain_stats_plots Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/rain_stats_plots.git  Repository commit hash: 76c806dd0e7263d75da5c6ed65e0625c5482c031 License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
 Code to generate plots and analyses for this paper: cacti_overview_paper Repository cloning location:  https://code.arm.gov/lasso/lasso-cacti/cacti_notebook_library.git  Repository commit hash: 27e46446cf8695f4eaa709083905d7a228c48fd5 (on branch cacti_overview_paper) License: This work is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License,  https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/ . 
   
 Acknowledgements 
 The authors thank participants of the LASSO Expansion Workshop held at the National Center of Atmospheric Research in May 2019 (Gustafson et al., 2019,    http://www.arm.gov/publications/programdocs/doe-sc-arm-19-023.pdf ), and in particular, the white paper authors that proposed the deep convection scenario that has evolved into LASSO-CACTI: Hugh Morrison, Ann Fridlind, Scott Giangrande, Adam Varble, Scott Collis, Zhe Feng, Daniel Hernandez-Deckers, Matthew Kumjian, Sonia Lasher-Trapp, Toshi Matsui, Mariko Oue, Glen Romine, Greg Thompson, Marcus van Lier-Walqui, and Guang Zhang. We also thank the dozens of ARM staff required to achieve LASSO-CACTI, without whom we would not be able to run and make available the LASSO-CACTI dataset. Portions of the work were performed at Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL). Battelle Memorial Institute operates PNNL under contract DEAC05-76RL01830, and at Brookhaven National Laboratory via a subcontract through PNNL. Brookhaven National Laboratory, Brookhaven Science Associates, LLC operates under contract DE-SC0012704 with the U.S. DOE. The Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility at the Oak Ridge National Laboratory is supported by the Office of Science of the U.S. Department of Energy under Contract No. DE-AC05-00OR22725. Computing resources for LASSO simulations provided by the Atmospheric Radiation Measurement user facility with additional operational support from the Oak Ridge Leadership Computing Facility. Data provided by NSF-NCAR/EOL under the sponsorship of the National Science Foundation,  https://data.eol.ucar.edu/ . Generated using Copernicus Climate Change Service information. Neither the European Commission nor ECMWF is responsible for any use that may be made of the Copernicus information or data it contains. SatCORPS data provided by the Satellite Cloud and Radiative Property Retrieval System team at NASA Langley Research Center. NOAA Geostationary Operational Environmental Satellites (GOES) 16 was accessed on 14 Jun 2024 from  https://registry.opendata.aws/noaa-goes . A portion of this work used code generously provided by Brian Blaylock's GOES-2-go python package ( https://github.com/blaylockbk/goes2go ). The publisher by accepting the paper for publication acknowledges that the United States Government retains a nonexclusive, paid-up, irrevocable, worldwide license to publish or reproduce the published form of this paper, or allow others to do so, for United States Government purposes.

Some figures were developed further in software such as Adobe Illustrator to adjust line thicknesses, panel layout, and labeling; these edits maintained the scientific integrity of the plots. Questions can be addressed to lasso@arm.gov. CACTI occurred in the region of Córdoba, Argentina from late 2018 through early 2019.
